rationale: Consumer byte-identity holds. FindOrphanedClosedBeads keeps its exact signature; its three call sites (complete/complete.go:111, cmd/mindspec/next.go:459, doctor/orphaned_beads.go:16) are untouched (git diff main vs these files: zero orphan-related hits). The wrapper is `orphans, _ := ScanOrphanedClosedBeads(...); return orphans` — identical fail-open discard, and the new mixed-list test proves the wrapper still returns a later provable orphan when an earlier bead's ancestry check errors, matching pre-refactor behavior exactly. reconcilePendingRefutations now calls `uncoveredPendingObligations` + `refuse(err.Error())`; the three error message strings (unreadable metadata, corrupt pending, corrupt refuted, malformed entry) are copied verbatim into the new shared core, so refuse() produces byte-identical guard failures. The settle loop (build toSatisfy from uncovered entries) is a mechanical rename of the same coverage-filtered walk. Inspected git diff for both _test.go files and mindspec_executor.go: all changes are purely additive test funcs appended at file end, or comment-only lines in the executor (executable lines identical). No pre-existing test's assertions were edited. All five consumer suites (lifecycle, complete, executor, doctor, cmd/mindspec) pass with -count=1; build is clean; worktree is clean aside from the review dir.
